denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 03/14/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W102296510          Group #: GRP-1290
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-143751
Claim #: CLM-2026-203371
Date of Service (requested): 02/22/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Northline Medical Group

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 02/24/2026 for the requested service.: Apalutamide plus androgen deprivation therapy
Diagnosis (as submitted): [REDACTED]
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0110 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0110 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Clinical criteria for Apalutamide plus androgen deprivation therapy are not met per the cited utilization guideline module for Metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (C61). The request does not satisfy threshold requirements in the referenced criteria set.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with guideline mis cite. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director before filing a written appeal.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W102296510) and reference UM-2026-143751. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department

This determination was made by Dr. J. Smith, Medical Director.
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (C61). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Apalutamide plus androgen deprivation therapy recommended as the next appropriate step. Rising PSA 18 ng/mL on ADT alone; bone scan negative. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that guideline mis cite bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
